Details: Two papers investigated the removal of 4-nonylphenol from water by electrochemical techniques. A potential difference of 0.7 V can achieve 38 to 100 percent removal of 4-nonylphenol. Percent removal is a function of the initial concentration and the reaction time. A reaction time of 10 min achieved 100 percent removal when the initial concentration was 1.1 mg/L 4-nonylphenol. At an initial concentration of 22 mg/L, a reaction time of 60 min achieved 65 percent removal.
